title: ASP Schema Bundle
description: Resolved bundle of all ASP schemas for code generation.

Discovery__search_request.description: Request to search for providers matching the given filters.

filters.title: Provider Filter
filters.description: Filter criteria for provider discovery searches.

max_service_minutes.description: Maximum acceptable service time in minutes.

is_promoted.description: Whether the provider is currently being promoted.

Discovery__search_response.description: Paginated list of providers matching the search criteria.

items.title: Provider
items.description: A service provider available for discovery and ordering.

rating_count.description: Number of ratings/reviews. Gives agents confidence to recommend: '4.8 stars from 2,300 reviews'.

is_open_now.description: Whether the provider is currently accepting orders.

is_promoted.description: Whether the provider is currently being promoted.

tags.description: Free-form tags relevant to this provider, e.g. 'fast food', 'gourmet', 'date night', 'romantic'.

operating_hours.description: Weekly operating schedule. Omit if provider is always available or schedule is unknown.

items.title: Operating Hours Entry
items.description: A single day's operating window for a provider.

open_time.description: Opening time in HH:MM (24-hour local time).

close_time.description: Closing time in HH:MM (24-hour local time). Use '23:59' for midnight close.

next_opens_at.description: ISO 8601 datetime when a currently closed provider next opens. Allows agents to say 'opens at 11am tomorrow' without parsing the full schedule.

images.description: Multiple images for different contexts (thumbnail for search, banner for detail page, etc.).

items.title: Image
items.description: Image reference with URL and optional dimensions.

alt_text.description: Accessible alt text for the image.

type.description: Purpose of this image. Helps clients pick the right asset for context (e.g. thumbnail for search results, banner for detail page).

Catalog__catalog_request.description: Request to retrieve a provider's catalog.

provider_id.description: Identifier of the provider whose catalog to retrieve.
Identifier of the provider this catalog belongs to.

items.title: Catalog Section
items.description: A categorized section of a provider's catalog (e.g. 'Appetizers', 'Sedans', 'Suites').
Items in this section.

is_featured.description: Whether this section is currently featured.

last_updated.description: Timestamp of the last catalog update.

Catalog__reorder_request.description: Request to reorder from a previous order. Returns current availability and pricing for the original items.

order_id.description: The previous order to reorder from.

provider_id.description: The provider of the original order.

scheduled_for.description: Optional scheduled time for the reorder. Integrates with available_for_scheduling on items.

Catalog__reorder_response.description: Current availability and pricing for items from the original order.

items.description: Items from the original order with current availability and pricing.

items.title: Catalog Item
items.description: A single item in a provider's catalog with price and modifiers.

modifier_groups.description: Available customization groups for this item.

items.title: Modifier Group
items.description: A group of modifier options for a catalog item (e.g. 'Size', 'Toppings').

title.description: Display title for this modifier group.

required.description: Whether the user must select an option from this group.

min_selections.description: Minimum number of options the user must select.

max_selections.description: Maximum number of options the user may select.

items.title: Modifier Option
items.description: A single selectable option within a modifier group (e.g. 'Large', 'Extra Cheese').

price_delta_cents.description: Price adjustment in cents when this option is selected.

is_available.description: Whether this item is currently available for ordering.

is_featured.description: Whether this item is currently featured.

tags.description: Free-form tags relevant to this item, e.g. 'fast food', 'gourmet', 'date night', 'romantic'.

images.description: Multiple images for different contexts (thumbnail for list view, hero for detail page, etc.).

unavailable_items.description: Items from the original order that are no longer available.

title.description: Display name of the unavailable item.

reason.description: Why the item is unavailable, e.g. 'Out of stock', 'No longer on menu', 'Provider closed'.

Fulfillment__fulfillment_checkout.description: UCP checkout extended with fulfillment, item customization, and loyalty discounts.

allOf.name: dev.ucp.shopping.checkout
allOf.title: Checkout Response
allOf.description: Base checkout schema. Extensions compose onto this using allOf.

id.description: Unique identifier of the checkout session.

line_items.description: List of line items being checked out.

full_name.description: Optional, buyer's full name (if first_name or last_name fields are present they take precedence).

status.description: Checkout state indicating the current phase and required action. See Checkout Status lifecycle documentation for state transition details.

items.title: Message
items.description: Container for error, warning, or info messages.

code.description: Error code. Possible values include: missing, invalid, out_of_stock, payment_declined, requires_sign_in, requires_3ds, requires_identity_linking. Freeform codes also allowed.

path.description: RFC 9535 JSONPath to the component the message refers to (e.g., $.items[1]).

severity.description: Declares who resolves this error. 'recoverable': agent can fix via API. 'requires_buyer_input': merchant requires information their API doesn't support collecting programmatically (checkout incomplete). 'requires_buyer_review': buyer must authorize before order placement due to policy, regulatory, or entitlement rules (checkout complete). Errors with 'requires_*' severity contribute to 'status: requires_escalation'.

code.description: Warning code. Machine-readable identifier for the warning type (e.g., final_sale, prop65, fulfillment_changed, age_restricted, etc.).

content.description: Human-readable warning message that MUST be displayed.

path.description: RFC 9535 JSONPath to the component the message refers to.

messages.description: List of messages with error and info about the checkout session state.

type.description: Type of link. Well-known values: `privacy_policy`, `terms_of_service`, `refund_policy`, `shipping_policy`, `faq`. Consumers SHOULD handle unknown values gracefully by displaying them using the `title` field or omitting the link.

url.description: The actual URL pointing to the content to be displayed.

title.description: Optional display text for the link. When provided, use this instead of generating from type.

links.description: Links to be displayed by the platform (Privacy Policy, TOS). Mandatory for legal compliance.

expires_at.description: RFC 3339 expiry timestamp. Default TTL is 6 hours from creation if not sent.

continue_url.description: URL for checkout handoff and session recovery. MUST be provided when status is requires_escalation. See specification for format and availability requirements.

id.description: The unique identifier for this handler instance within the payment.handlers. Used by payment instruments to reference which handler produced them.

name.description: The specification name using reverse-DNS format. For example, dev.ucp.delegate_payment.

spec.description: A URI pointing to the technical specification or schema that defines how this handler operates.

config_schema.description: A URI pointing to a JSON Schema used to validate the structure of the config object.

items.description: A URI pointing to a schema definition (e.g., JSON Schema) used to validate the structure of the instrument object.

config.description: A dictionary containing provider-specific configuration details, such as merchant IDs, supported networks, or gateway credentials.

handlers.description: Processing configurations that define how payment instruments can be collected. Each handler specifies a tokenization or payment collection strategy.

selected_instrument_id.description: The id of the currently selected payment instrument from the instruments array. Set by the agent when submitting payment, and echoed back by the merchant in finalized state.

items.title: Payment Instrument
items.description: Matches a specific instrument type based on validation logic.

oneOf.title: Card Payment Instrument
oneOf.description: A basic card payment instrument with visible card details. Can be inherited by a handler's instrument schema to define handler-specific display details or more complex credential structures.

allOf.title: Payment Instrument Base
allOf.description: The base definition for any payment instrument. It links the instrument to a specific Merchant configuration (handler_id) and defines common fields like billing address.

id.description: A unique identifier for this instrument instance, assigned by the Agent. Used to reference this specific instrument in the 'payment.selected_instrument_id' field.

handler_id.description: The unique identifier for the handler instance that produced this instrument. This corresponds to the 'id' field in the Payment Handler definition.

type.description: The broad category of the instrument (e.g., 'card', 'tokenized_card'). Specific schemas will constrain this to a constant value.

billing_address.description: The billing address associated with this payment method.

credential.title: Payment Credential
credential.description: Container for sensitive payment data. Use the specific schema matching the 'type' field.

oneOf.title: Token Credential Response
oneOf.description: Base token credential schema. Concrete payment handlers may extend this schema with additional fields and define their own constraints.

type.description: The specific type of token produced by the handler (e.g., 'stripe_token').

oneOf.title: Card Credential
oneOf.description: A card credential containing sensitive payment card details including raw Primary Account Numbers (PANs). This credential type MUST NOT be used for checkout, only with payment handlers that tokenize or encrypt credentials. CRITICAL: Both parties handling CardCredential (sender and receiver) MUST be PCI DSS compliant. Transmission MUST use HTTPS/TLS with strong cipher suites.

type.description: The credential type identifier for card credentials.

card_number_type.description: The type of card number. Network tokens are preferred with fallback to FPAN. See PCI Scope for more details.

expiry_month.description: The month of the card's expiration date (1-12).

expiry_year.description: The year of the card's expiration date.

eci_value.description: Electronic Commerce Indicator / Security Level Indicator provided with network tokens.

type.description: Indicates this is a card payment instrument.

brand.description: The card brand/network (e.g., visa, mastercard, amex).

last_digits.description: Last 4 digits of the card number.

expiry_month.description: The month of the card's expiration date (1-12).

expiry_year.description: The year of the card's expiration date.

rich_text_description.description: An optional rich text description of the card to display to the user (e.g., 'Visa ending in 1234, expires 12/2025').

rich_card_art.description: An optional URI to a rich image representing the card (e.g., card art provided by the issuer).

instruments.description: The payment instruments available for this payment. Each instrument is associated with a specific handler via the handler_id field. Handlers can extend the base payment_instrument schema to add handler-specific fields.

order.description: Details about an order created for this checkout session.
order.title: Order Confirmation

permalink_url.description: Permalink to access the order on merchant site.

estimated_service_minutes.description: Estimated service time in minutes from order confirmation.

address.description: Address for the fulfillment (delivery destination, pickup location, etc.).

instructions.description: Special fulfillment instructions (e.g. 'Leave at door', 'Meet at lobby').

is_asap.description: Whether to fulfill as soon as possible vs. a scheduled time.

fees.description: Itemized fees beyond the base price. Lets agents explain cost breakdowns: 'Delivery fee $5, service fee $3.'

items.title: Fee
items.description: An individual fee line item. Itemizes charges beyond the base price so agents can explain 'why does it cost this much?'

type.description: Fee category. Base types cover common cross-vertical fees; domain profiles may define additional types.

label.description: Human-readable display name, e.g. 'Delivery fee', 'Bag charge', 'Surge pricing'.

amount_cents.description: Fee amount in minor currency units (cents).

tip.description: Optional gratuity for the service provider or driver.
tip.title: Tip

percentage.description: Tip as a percentage of the order total. Present when type is 'percentage'.

amount_cents.description: Resolved tip amount in minor currency units (cents).

scheduled_for.description: Requested service time as ISO 8601 datetime. Used when is_asap is false, e.g. 'Deliver at 7pm tonight'.

available_time_slots.description: Time slots the provider offers. Agent presents these as choices: 'They have slots at 6pm, 6:30pm, or 7pm.'

is_available.description: Whether this slot is still available for booking.

line_items.description: Cart line items with optional per-item customization.

allOf.title: Line Item Response
allOf.description: Line item object. Expected to use the currency of the parent object.

id.description: Should be recognized by both the Platform, and the Business. For Google it should match the id provided in the "id" field in the product feed.

price.description: Unit price in minor (cents) currency units.

quantity.description: Quantity of the item being purchased.

parent_id.description: Parent line item identifier for any nested structures.

allOf.title: Item Customization
allOf.description: Per-item customization extending UCP line items with special instructions and modifier selections.

modifiers.description: Selected modifier option IDs from the item's modifier groups.

loyalty.description: Loyalty tier discount applied to this checkout.
loyalty.title: Loyalty Discount

loyalty_tier.description: User's loyalty tier as defined by the marketplace, e.g. 'gold', 'premium', 'VIP', 'level_3'.

loyalty_discount_percent.description: Discount percentage applied based on loyalty tier.

OrderTracking__tracked_order.description: UCP order extended with fulfillment-specific status and ETA.

allOf.name: dev.ucp.shopping.order
allOf.title: Order
allOf.description: Order schema with immutable line items, buyer-facing fulfillment expectations, and append-only event logs.

webhook_url.description: URL where merchant sends order lifecycle events (webhooks).

ucp.title: UCP Order Response
ucp.description: UCP metadata for order responses. No payment handlers needed post-purchase.

version.description: UCP protocol version in YYYY-MM-DD format.

items.title: Capability (Response)
items.description: Capability reference in responses. Only name/version required to confirm active capabilities.

name.description: Stable capability identifier in reverse-domain notation (e.g., dev.ucp.shopping.checkout). Used in capability negotiation.

schema.description: URL to JSON Schema for this capability's payload.

extends.description: Parent capability this extends. Present for extensions, absent for root capabilities.

config.description: Capability-specific configuration (structure defined by each capability).

permalink_url.description: Permalink to access the order on merchant site.

quantity.description: Quantity tracking. Both total and fulfilled are derived from events.

status.description: Derived status: fulfilled if quantity.fulfilled == quantity.total, partial if quantity.fulfilled > 0, otherwise processing.

parent_id.description: Parent line item identifier for any nested structures.

line_items.description: Immutable line items — source of truth for what was ordered.

items.title: Expectation
items.description: Buyer-facing fulfillment expectation representing logical groupings of items (e.g., 'package'). Can be split, merged, or adjusted post-order to set buyer expectations for when/how items arrive.

quantity.description: Quantity of this item in this expectation.

line_items.description: Which line items and quantities are in this expectation.

extended_address.description: An address extension such as an apartment number, C/O or alternative name.

address_locality.description: The locality in which the street address is, and which is in the region. For example, Mountain View.

address_region.description: The region in which the locality is, and which is in the country. Required for applicable countries (i.e. state in US, province in CA). For example, California or another appropriate first-level Administrative division.

address_country.description: The country. Recommended to be in 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 format, for example "US". For backward compatibility, a 3-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-3 country code such as "SGP" or a full country name such as "Singapore" can also be used.

first_name.description: Optional. First name of the contact associated with the address.

last_name.description: Optional. Last name of the contact associated with the address.

full_name.description: Optional. Full name of the contact associated with the address (if first_name or last_name fields are present they take precedence).

phone_number.description: Optional. Phone number of the contact associated with the address.

description.description: Human-readable delivery description (e.g., 'Arrives in 5-8 business days').

fulfillable_on.description: When this expectation can be fulfilled: 'now' or ISO 8601 timestamp for future date (backorder, pre-order).

expectations.description: Buyer-facing groups representing when/how items will be delivered. Can be split, merged, or adjusted post-order.

items.title: Fulfillment Event
items.description: Append-only fulfillment event representing an actual shipment. References line items by ID.

occurred_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when this fulfillment event occurred.

type.description: Fulfillment event type. Common values include: processing (preparing to ship), shipped (handed to carrier), in_transit (in delivery network), delivered (received by buyer), failed_attempt (delivery attempt failed), canceled (fulfillment canceled), undeliverable (cannot be delivered), returned_to_sender (returned to merchant).

line_items.description: Which line items and quantities are fulfilled in this event.

tracking_number.description: Carrier tracking number (required if type != processing).

tracking_url.description: URL to track this shipment (required if type != processing).

description.description: Human-readable description of the shipment status or delivery information (e.g., 'Delivered to front door', 'Out for delivery').

events.description: Append-only event log of actual shipments. Each event references line items by ID.

fulfillment.description: Fulfillment data: buyer expectations and what actually happened.

items.title: Adjustment
items.description: Append-only event that exists independently of fulfillment. Typically represents money movements but can be any post-order change. Polymorphic type that can optionally reference line items.

type.description: Type of adjustment (open string). Typically money-related like: refund, return, credit, price_adjustment, dispute, cancellation. Can be any value that makes sense for the merchant's business.

occurred_at.description: RFC 3339 timestamp when this adjustment occurred.

line_items.description: Which line items and quantities are affected (optional).

amount.description: Amount in minor units (cents) for refunds, credits, price adjustments (optional).

description.description: Human-readable reason or description (e.g., 'Defective item', 'Customer requested').

adjustments.description: Append-only event log of money movements (refunds, returns, credits, disputes, cancellations, etc.) that exist independently of fulfillment.

display_text.description: Text to display against the amount. Should reflect appropriate method (e.g., 'Shipping', 'Delivery').

amount.description: If type == total, sums subtotal - discount + fulfillment + tax + fee. Should be >= 0. Amount in minor (cents) currency units.

fulfillment_status.description: Current fulfillment status and tracking information.
fulfillment_status.title: Fulfillment Status

order_id.description: Identifier of the order being tracked.

estimated_service_minutes.description: Updated estimated service time in minutes.

updated_at.description: Timestamp of the last status update.

agent_location.description: Real-time agent location for live tracking.

history.description: Ordered timeline of status transitions. Enables agents to narrate the full journey: 'Accepted at 7:01, picked up at 7:15.'

status.description: The status at this point in time.

note.description: Optional human-readable context, e.g. 'Driver stuck in traffic'.

is_delayed.description: Whether the order is running behind the original estimate. Allows agents to proactively warn: 'Heads up, your order is running late.'

delay_minutes.description: How many minutes behind the original estimate. e.g. 'Running about 10 minutes late.'

OrderTracking__status_update_event.title: Status Update Event
OrderTracking__status_update_event.description: Webhook payload sent when order status changes. Push model — the marketplace sends this to the agent/platform.

order_id.description: Identifier of the order whose status changed.

Reviews__submit_review_request.description: Request to submit a review after service completion.

Reviews__get_reviews_request.description: Request to retrieve reviews for a provider.

provider_id.description: The provider whose reviews to retrieve.

Reviews__get_reviews_response.description: Paginated list of reviews for a provider.

items.title: Review
items.description: A post-service review submitted by a customer.

order_id.description: The order this review is for.

average_rating.description: Provider's average rating across all reviews.

total_reviews.description: Total number of reviews for this provider.

has_more.description: Whether more pages of reviews are available.

Streaming__stream_subscribe.description: Client-to-server message to start receiving tracking events for an order.

order_id.description: Identifier of the order to track.

token.description: Bearer token for authentication. Sent in the first message since WebSocket handshake does not support custom HTTP headers in browsers.

Streaming__stream_unsubscribe.description: Client-to-server message to stop receiving tracking events for an order.

order_id.description: Identifier of the order to stop tracking.

Streaming__stream_event.description: Server-to-client event envelope. See tracking_stream_event.json for the full schema.
Streaming__stream_event.title: Tracking Stream Event

event_type.description: Discriminator for routing the message to the correct handler.

order_id.description: Identifier of the order being tracked. Present on all events except heartbeat.

timestamp.description: Server timestamp when the event was emitted.

oneOf.description: Continuous agent location and ETA update. Sent every 3-5 seconds during the en_route phase.

status.description: Current order status at time of location update.

heading.description: Compass heading in degrees (0 = north, 90 = east). Useful for rotating a map marker.

oneOf.description: Status transition event. Sent when the order moves between lifecycle stages.
Keep-alive heartbeat. Sent every 30 seconds to maintain the connection.

Personalization__profile_response.description: The user's profile including preferences, loyalty status, and order history.
Personalization__profile_response.title: User Profile

loyalty_tier.description: Current loyalty program tier as defined by the marketplace, e.g. 'gold', 'premium', 'VIP', 'level_3'.

preferred_categories.description: User's preferred service categories for personalized recommendations.

items.title: ServiceCategory
items.description: Provider category. Open string — see domain profiles for vertical-specific values.

order_history.description: Recent order history for reorder suggestions and personalization.

items.title: Order History Entry
items.description: A single entry in the user's order history.
Items ordered.

ordered_at.description: Timestamp when the order was placed.

Personalization__promotions_request.description: Request for personalized promotions, optionally scoped to a provider.

provider_id.description: Scope promotions to a specific provider.

limit.description: Maximum number of promotions to return.

Personalization__promotions_response.description: List of personalized promotions for the user.

items.title: Promotion
items.description: A personalized promotion targeted to a specific user.

applicable_provider_id.description: Provider this promotion applies to, if scoped.

minimum_order_cents.description: Minimum order value to qualify, in cents.

Asp.title: ASP Protocol Metadata
Asp.description: Defines the top-level protocol metadata used in discovery profiles and capability responses.

services.description: Map of service name to service definition.

spec.description: URL to the human-readable spec page.

endpoint.description: Base URL for the REST API.

schema.description: URL to the OpenRPC specification (JSON format) defining available MCP tools.

agentCard.description: URL to the A2A agent card (JSON).

schema.description: URL to the AsyncAPI specification defining WebSocket channels.

Capability.title: Capability Declaration
Capability.description: Describes a single capability or extension in a discovery profile.

spec.description: Link to the human-readable spec page.

extends.description: Name of the capability this extends. Only set for extensions.
